Object.Name: Zigbee Group
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object enables Zigbee group management for collective device control and scene automation. Groups allow simultaneous control of multiple devices (lights, switches, etc.) with a single command, reducing network traffic and ensuring synchronized operation. Supports dynamic group membership management, scene storage and recall, and common control commands (on/off, level, color). Essential for lighting zones, room control, and automation scenarios in smart home and commercial building deployments. Implements Zigbee Groups cluster (0x0004) and Scenes cluster (0x0005) functionality. Each instance represents one logical group with unique 16-bit Group ID.

Item.Name: Group ID
Item.Description: 16-bit group identifier (0x0001-0xFFF7). Must be unique within network. Used for group addressing in ZCL commands. Reserved ranges: 0x0000 (not allowed), 0xFFF8-0xFFFF (special broadcast groups). Configure before adding members.

Item.Name: Group Name
Item.Description: Human-readable group name for identification and display (e.g., "Living Room Lights", "Kitchen Zone", "All Lights"). Maximum 16 characters. Used in user interfaces and automation rules. Should be descriptive and unique.

Item.Name: Member Devices
Item.Description: IEEE addresses (64-bit hex) of devices in group. Multiple resource instances, one per member. Automatically updated when devices join/leave group. Used for membership tracking and group management.

Item.Name: Member Count
Item.Description: Total number of devices currently in group. Updates automatically when members added/removed. Empty groups (count=0) can be pre-created for future use. Monitor to ensure group commands reach all intended devices.

Item.Name: Scene Count
Item.Description: Number of scenes defined for this group. Scenes store device states (on/off, brightness, color) for instant recall. Maximum typically 16 scenes per group. Each scene has unique ID (0-254). Used for lighting presets and automation.

Item.Name: Current Scene
Item.Description: Currently active scene ID (0-254). 0 indicates no scene active. Updated when Scene Recall is executed. Can be written to recall specific scene. Used to track and restore lighting states.

Item.Name: Add Member
Item.Description: Execute to add device to group. Argument: 64-bit IEEE address (8 bytes). Sends ZCL Add Group command to device. Device must support Groups cluster. Verify membership via Member Count after execution. Can add multiple devices by executing repeatedly.

Item.Name: Remove Member
Item.Description: Execute to remove device from group. Argument: 64-bit IEEE address (8 bytes). Sends ZCL Remove Group command to device. Device will no longer respond to group commands. Useful for reconfiguring zones or decommissioning devices.

Item.Name: Scene Recall
Item.Description: Execute to recall stored scene. Argument: scene ID (0-254, 1 byte). Restores all group member states to scene settings instantly (<1 second). Used for lighting presets, automation, and mood setting. Scene must exist or command fails.

Item.Name: Scene Store
Item.Description: Execute to store current group state as scene. Argument: scene ID (0-254, 1 byte). Captures on/off, brightness, color state of all members. Overwrites scene if ID exists. Used to create lighting presets. Increment Scene Count after storage.

Item.Name: On
Item.Description: Execute to turn on all devices in group. Sends ZCL On command (cluster 0x0006, command 0x01) to group address. All devices respond simultaneously. Applies to lights, switches, outlets. Instant synchronized activation.

Item.Name: Off
Item.Description: Execute to turn off all devices in group. Sends ZCL Off command (cluster 0x0006, command 0x00) to group address. All devices deactivate simultaneously. Master switch functionality for zones or entire system.

Item.Name: Toggle
Item.Description: Execute to toggle all devices in group. Sends ZCL Toggle command (cluster 0x0006, command 0x02). Devices currently on will turn off, off devices turn on. Note: Mixed states may result in unexpected behavior. Best used with synchronized groups.

Item.Name: Set Level
Item.Description: Execute to set brightness level for all dimmable devices in group. Argument: level 0-254 (1 byte). 0=minimum, 254=maximum brightness. Sends ZCL Move to Level command (cluster 0x0008). Instant or timed transition depending on device capabilities.

Item.Name: Set Color
Item.Description: Execute to set color for all color-capable devices in group. Arguments: hue (0-254), saturation (0-254) - 2 bytes total. Sends ZCL Move to Hue and Saturation command (cluster 0x0300). Only affects devices with color control capability.
